hints.text: **Yellow outer lines**, middle white or yellow

shared_with.diff: No English on signs + black Google car → Jordan.
Oman's pickup antenna/pole details + generally more mountainous SE.

hints.text: Gen3: **white car with black roof racks**
hints.uniq_note: The only Middle-Eastern country with a white Google car — white cars exist on other continents, so pair with Arabic+English signage.

hints.text: Gen4: **white pickup w/ trekker camera** (often fully blurred) or black car w/ visible brake light

shared_with.diff: UAE: front antenna in the **middle** of the cabin; Qatar: on the **left**.
Check the antenna + Oman's mountains/kerb styles.
Pickup antenna position + Doha skyline → Qatar.

hints.text: Residential houses of **light-coloured stone**; **reddish sidewalk stones**
**Black-white striped signposts** (in the Middle East also Oman); nearly all kerbs striped (b&w or black-yellow)

shared_with.diff: Oman: brown wooden poles similar; more mountainous; different plate shape.

hints.text: **Divided roads + roundabouts** everywhere; residential roads often **unpaved** despite the wealth
Poles: **dark-brown wood, 3 insulators symmetric on a horizontal bar**

shared_with.note: Jordanian lookalikes are **grey concrete** with wider-spaced insulators.

hints.text: **Camera rifts** occasionally (most common in Albania, Montenegro, Senegal)
Coverage mostly **northeast** (only exception with car coverage: Sir Baniyas Island; other remote spots = trekkers)
**Dubai skyline** w/ Burj Khalifa; mountains ONLY in the far east; N-Ras-al-Khaimah = light mountains east; mountains west + flat east = Gulf of Oman coast
Rare **white car w/ visible side mirrors** + sparse vegetation
Gen4 **sidewalk trekker** (sometimes black hair visible) incl. old town; **blue street signs w/ narrow metal border** specific to Dubai
**Trekker spotlights**: Al Ain (2 forts + zoo, far from car coverage); racetrack east of Abu Dhabi; remote dune trekkers in the far south (Taraq↔Alyhyali)
